One such model is the Segway GT3 Pro SuperScooter that is dropping down at $2,499.99 shipped in its second-ever discount, beating out Amazon by $200. This is the brand’s most premium e-scooter that has mostly been keeping to its $2,700 full price since hitting the market in August 2025. We saw it get its first official price cut last month during the brand’s Prime Day savings event, and the deal is returning here nearly a month later for a second chance at $200 savings to the lowest price we have tracked. Head below to learn more about it and browse through the entire sale lineup.

items.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
items.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies in small, private communities powered by messaging, photo sharing, and AI features designed to strengthen real-world relationships.

items.title: Clearance pricing live on GoPro’s HERO12 Black Creator combo with battery grip, light mod, more at nearly $300 off launch price
items.summary: As part of its ongoing Black Friday in July sale, Best Buy is offering GoPro’s HERO12 Black Creator Edition bundle for $302.99 shipped. This originally launched at $599, and Amazon last had it listed for $550 before it went out of stock there once newer models hit the shelves. Best Buy is clearing out the stock here by taking $247 off its listed price and landing the bundle $47 below the previous $349.99 all-time low we tracked. This bundle delivers some serious value at this price, so snag it if you were looking to buy a new action camera.

items.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)
items.summary: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series D, and Murphy has had a front-row seat as the company went from a pre-revenue, […]
